title: centaurea montana subsp. variegata
intro: Centaurea montana subsp. variegata, also known as the Mountain Cornflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Centaurea montana subsp. variegata include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Centaurea montana subsp. variegata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to various parts of the body.
